briefdescription.para: Creates an uninitialized LongPressGestureDetector; this can be initialized with LongPressGestureDetector::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized LongPressGestureDetector handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0
This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: This move constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This move assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.4
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Sets the number of touches required.

detaileddescription.para: The number of touches corresponds to the number of fingers a user has on the screen. This sets the minimum and maximum touches to the input parameter.

@SINCE_1_0.0
touches
Touches required
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The default is '1'.

briefdescription.para: Sets the minimum and maximum touches required.

detaileddescription.para: The number of touches corresponds to the number of fingers a user has on the screen.

@SINCE_1_0.0
minTouches
Minimum Touches required
maxTouches
Maximum Touches required
The gesture detector has been initialized.
The default is '1'.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the minimum number of touches required.
Retrieves the maximum number of touches required.
This signal is emitted when the specified long press is detected on the attached actor.

detaileddescription.para:
By default, this would create a gesture detector that requires only one touch. @SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized LongPressGestureDetector with the number of touches required.

detaileddescription.para:
A long press gesture will be emitted from this detector if the number of fingers touching the screen is equal to the touches required. @SINCE_1_0.0
touchesRequired
The number of touches required
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized LongPressGestureDetector with the minimum and maximum number of touches required.

detaileddescription.para:
A long press gesture will be emitted from this detector if the number of fingers touching the screen falls between the minimum and maximum touches set. @SINCE_1_0.0
minTouches
The minimum number of touches required
maxTouches
The maximum number of touches required
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to LongPressGestureDetector handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a LongPressGestureDetector object, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
Handle to an object
Handle to a LongPressGestureDetector object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: This class emits a signals when a long press gesture occurs that meets the requirements set by the application. @SINCE_1_0.0.

detaileddescription.para:
For any valid long press, two signals will be emitted:
First identifying the beginning (state = GestureState::STARTED) i.e. when fingers held down for the required time.
Second identifying the ending (state = GestureState::FINISHED) i.e. when fingers are released.

LongPressGesture
Signals
Signal Name
Method
longPressDetected
DetectedSignal()
